mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance on modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but it may require careful resource management and tuning to maximize performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the system PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary for your requirements.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to point to the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are ready and operational.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this may result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d-deployment

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the llm-d deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all its resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade the llm-d deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n llm-d -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase=="Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-deployment-v0.5.1.yaml

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> This command drains the nodes to prepare for upgrade.
> This command applies the new deployment configuration.
> Verify that the new pods are running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n llm-d -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase=="Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image cannot be found.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Review the application logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'FailedScheduling'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There are no available nodes that match the pod's resource requests.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in your deployment and adjust them accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.5.0, no specific migration steps are required.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
